message:
test: Add unit test framework for pure functions

- Create extract-functions.mjs to dynamically extract JS from heredoc
- Add platform-detection.test.mjs with 17 tests for detectPlatform()
- Add content-validation.test.mjs with 31 tests for validation functions
- Add run-tests.sh helper script
- Integrate unit tests into CI pipeline (runs before install-test)

Tests cover:
- Platform URL detection (iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, Google Drive)
- Content-Type validation (image types, HTML detection)
- Magic bytes detection (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, HEIC, BMP, TIFF)
- validateImageContent error handling

All 48 tests passing with Node.js 20+ native test runner.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@
 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.5.2","title":"Change default to preserve original bytes (v3 behavior)","description":"## What

Change v3 default behavior from MozJPEG compression to preserving original bytes.

## Why This Change

1. **Quality preservation**: UI screenshots shouldn't be recompressed
2. **Performance**: Skip unnecessary processing when not needed
3. **User expectations**: "Download image" should mean original image
4. **Opt-in processing**: Users who want compression can request it

## v2 Behavior (Current)
```bash
# Always processes through Sharp + MozJPEG
giil "https://..." 
# Output: Recompressed JPEG at quality 85
```

## v3 Behavior (New)
```bash
# Preserve original bytes by default
giil "https://..."
# Output: Original file (JPEG/PNG/HEIC as received)

# Opt-in to compression
giil "https://..." --optimize
# Output: MozJPEG-optimized JPEG

# Opt-in to format conversion
giil "https://..." --convert jpeg
# Output: Converted to JPEG (from HEIC/PNG)
```

## New Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--optimize` | Apply MozJPEG optimization |
| `--convert FORMAT` | Convert to format (jpeg, png, webp) |
| `--quality N` | JPEG quality (only with --optimize or --convert) |

## Implementation Changes

```javascript
async function processImage(buffer, options) {
    // v3: Only process if explicitly requested
    if (!options.optimize && !options.convert) {
        return buffer;  // Return original bytes
    }

    // Apply Sharp processing only when requested
    let pipeline = sharp(buffer);
    
    if (options.convert === 'jpeg' || options.optimize) {
        pipeline = pipeline.jpeg({
            quality: options.quality || 85,
            mozjpeg: options.optimize === true
        });
    }
    // ... handle other formats
    
    return pipeline.toBuffer();
}
```

## Migration Notes

Users relying on automatic compression must add `--optimize`:
```bash
# v2 equivalent in v3
giil "https://..." --optimize --quality 85
```

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.5.3","title":"Add debugging CLI flags (--print-url, --verbose, --trace)","description":"## What

Add debugging-focused CLI flags to help users troubleshoot issues.

## New Flags

### --print-url
Output the resolved direct URL instead of downloading.

```bash
$ giil "https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png?dl=0" --print-url
https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png?raw=1

$ giil "https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123" --print-url
https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=s0
```

**Use cases:**
- Debugging: see what URL giil is actually hitting
- Integration: pipe URL to other tools (curl, wget)
- Verification: confirm URL transformation is correct

### --verbose
Show detailed progress and error information.

```bash
$ giil "https://..." --verbose
[giil] Detected platform: gphotos
[giil] Launching Playwright (headless)
[giil] Navigating to: https://photos.google.com/share/...
[giil] Installing network interceptor
[giil] Captured CDN URL: lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=w1042-h862
[giil] Extracting base URL: lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx
[giil] Downloading full resolution with =s0 modifier
[giil] Downloaded 2.14 MB
[giil] Extracting EXIF datetime: 2026-01-03T14:22:45
[giil] Saved to: gphotos_20260103_142245.jpg
```

### --trace
Enable Playwright tracing for deep debugging.

```bash
$ giil "https://..." --trace
[giil] Playwright tracing enabled
[giil] Trace will be saved to: giil_trace_20260103_142245.zip
# ... normal operation ...
[giil] Trace saved: giil_trace_20260103_142245.zip
[giil] View trace: npx playwright show-trace giil_trace_20260103_142245.zip
```

**What tracing captures:**
- Screenshots at each action
- DOM snapshots
- Network requests/responses
- Console logs

### --debug-dir PATH
Specify where to save debug artifacts (default: current directory).

```bash
$ giil "https://..." --debug --debug-dir /tmp/giil-debug
[giil] Debug artifacts will be saved to: /tmp/giil-debug/
```

## Implementation

### Bash Layer
```bash
PRINT_URL=false
VERBOSE=false
TRACE=false
DEBUG_DIR=""

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --print-url)  PRINT_URL=true; shift ;;
        --verbose)    VERBOSE=true; shift ;;
        --trace)      TRACE=true; shift ;;
        --debug-dir)  DEBUG_DIR="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        # ... existing flags
    esac
done
```

### JavaScript Layer
```javascript
const verbose = process.argv.includes('--verbose');
const trace = process.argv.includes('--trace');

function log(msg) {
    if (verbose) console.error(`[giil] ${msg}`);
}
```

## Interaction with Existing Flags

| Flag Combo | Behavior |
|------------|----------|
| --print-url --json | URL in JSON format: `{"url": "..."}` |
| --verbose --json | Verbose to stderr, JSON to stdout |
| --debug --trace | Both artifacts saved |

## Priority

P3 - Nice to have for troubleshooting, not blocking for MVP.

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.6","title":"EPIC: Phase 6 - Testing Infrastructure","description":"## Overview

Build a comprehensive testing infrastructure with three layers: unit tests, replay tests, and live contract tests.

## Why Three Layers

| Layer | Speed | Reliability | Coverage |
|-------|-------|-------------|----------|
| Unit | Fast (<1s) | 100% deterministic | Logic only |
| Replay | Medium (1-5s) | 99% deterministic | Full flow, offline |
| Live | Slow (10-60s) | Variable | Real platforms |

## Layer 1: Unit Tests (Always in CI)

Test pure functions without network or browser:
- Platform detection (`detect_platform`, `detectPlatform`)
- URL normalization (`normalize_dropbox_url`, etc.)
- File ID extraction (`extractGoogleDriveFileId`)
- Content validation (MIME types, magic bytes)
- Output schema formatting

```bash
# Run unit tests
npm test -- --grep "unit"
```

## Layer 2: Replay Tests (Always in CI)

Use recorded HAR fixtures to test full extraction flow:
- Record network traffic from real sessions
- Replay with Playwright's HAR feature
- Deterministic, fast, works offline

```bash
# Record a fixture
RECORD_HAR=1 giil "https://..." --output fixtures/

# Run replay tests
npm test -- --grep "replay"
```

## Layer 3: Live Contract Tests (Scheduled/Optional)

Test against real URLs to catch platform changes:
- Gated behind `LIVE_TESTS=1` environment variable
- Run nightly or on-demand
- Accept occasional flakiness

```bash
# Run live tests
LIVE_TESTS=1 npm test -- --grep "live"
```

## CI Configuration

```yaml
jobs:
  unit-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: npm test -- --grep "unit|replay"

  live-tests:
    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: LIVE_TESTS=1 npm test -- --grep "live"
```

## Test URLs (For Live Tests)

Create dedicated public test shares for each platform:
- Dropbox: Public folder with test images
- Google Photos: Public album with test images
- Google Drive: Public file with "Anyone with link"
- iCloud: Existing test link

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests for all platform detection functions
- [ ] Replay fixtures for each platform
- [ ] Live tests with dedicated test shares
- [ ] CI runs unit+replay on every PR
- [ ] Nightly live test workflow

-{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.6.1","title":"Create unit test framework for pure functions","description":"## What

Set up a unit test framework for testing pure functions without network or browser.

## Scope

Test these functions in isolation:
- `detect_platform()` / `detectPlatform()` - Platform detection
- `normalize_dropbox_url()` - URL normalization
- `extractGoogleDriveFileId()` - File ID parsing
- `validateContentType()` - MIME type validation
- `validateMagicBytes()` - Binary header checking
- `formatOutput()` - JSON schema formatting
- `getUniqueFilename()` - Filename generation

## Test Framework Choice

**Option A: Node.js native test runner (recommended)**
```bash
# Node.js 18+ has built-in test runner
node --test tests/*.test.mjs
```

Pros:
- Zero dependencies
- Fast startup
- Native assertion library

**Option B: Vitest/Jest**
More features but adds dependencies to a zero-dep tool.

**Recommendation: Node.js native test runner**

## Implementation

### File Structure
```
scripts/
└── tests/
    ├── platform-detection.test.mjs
    ├── url-normalization.test.mjs
    ├── content-validation.test.mjs
    └── output-formatting.test.mjs
```

### Example Test: Platform Detection
```javascript
// scripts/tests/platform-detection.test.mjs
import { describe, it } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert';
import { detectPlatform } from '../../extractor-functions.mjs';

describe('detectPlatform', () => {
    it('detects iCloud share URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123'), 'icloud');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://www.icloud.com/photos/#abc123'), 'icloud');
    });

    it('detects Dropbox URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?dl=0'), 'dropbox');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png'), 'dropbox');
    });

    it('detects Google Photos URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123'), 'gphotos');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://photos.google.com/share/abc?key=xyz'), 'gphotos');
    });

    it('detects Google Drive URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://drive.google.com/file/d/xyz/view'), 'gdrive');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://drive.google.com/open?id=xyz'), 'gdrive');
    });

    it('returns unknown for unsupported URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://example.com/photo.jpg'), 'unknown');
    });
});
```

### Bash Function Testing

Extract bash functions and test with bats or shell assertions:

```bash
# scripts/tests/test-bash-detection.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source <(sed -n '/^detect_platform()/,/^}/p' ../../giil)

assert_eq() {
    [[ "$1" == "$2" ]] || { echo "FAIL: expected '$2', got '$1'"; exit 1; }
}

assert_eq "$(detect_platform 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc')" "icloud"
assert_eq "$(detect_platform 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png')" "dropbox"
echo "All bash tests passed"
```

## CI Integration

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
unit-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with: { node-version: '20' }
    - run: node --test scripts/tests/*.test.mjs
    - run: bash scripts/tests/test-bash-detection.sh
```

## Test Extraction Challenge

The functions live inside the heredoc. Options:
1. **Extract to separate file** - More modular but adds files
2. **Inline test generation** - Test script extracts functions dynamically
3. **E2E only** - Skip unit tests, rely on replay/live tests

**Recommendation:** Option 2 - dynamically extract functions for testing without adding files to the distribution.

## Scope

Test these functions in isolation:
- `detect_platform()` / `detectPlatform()` - Platform detection
- `normalize_dropbox_url()` - URL normalization
- `extractGoogleDriveFileId()` - File ID parsing
- `validateContentType()` - MIME type validation
- `validateMagicBytes()` - Binary header checking
- `formatOutput()` - JSON schema formatting


Pros:

## Implementation

### Example Test: Platform Detection
```javascript
// scripts/tests/platform-detection.test.mjs
import { describe, it } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert';
import { detectPlatform } from '../../extractor-functions.mjs';

describe('detectPlatform', () => {
    it('detects iCloud share URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123'), 'icloud');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://www.icloud.com/photos/#abc123'), 'icloud');
    });

    it('detects Dropbox URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?dl=0'), 'dropbox');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png'), 'dropbox');
    });

    it('detects Google Photos URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123'), 'gphotos');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://photos.google.com/share/abc?key=xyz'), 'gphotos');
    });

    it('detects Google Drive URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://drive.google.com/file/d/xyz/view'), 'gdrive');
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://drive.google.com/open?id=xyz'), 'gdrive');
    });

    it('returns unknown for unsupported URLs', () => {
        assert.equal(detectPlatform('https://example.com/photo.jpg'), 'unknown');
    });
});
```

### Bash Function Testing

Extract bash functions and test with bats or shell assertions:

```bash
# scripts/tests/test-bash-detection.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source <(sed -n '/^detect_platform()/,/^}/p' ../../giil)

assert_eq() {
    [[ "$1" == "$2" ]] || { echo "FAIL: expected '$2', got '$1'"; exit 1; }
}

assert_eq "$(detect_platform 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc')" "icloud"
assert_eq "$(detect_platform 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/xxx/photo.png')" "dropbox"
echo "All bash tests passed"
```

## CI Integration

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
unit-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with: { node-version: '20' }
    - run: node --test scripts/tests/*.test.mjs
    - run: bash scripts/tests/test-bash-detection.sh
```

## Test Extraction Challenge

The functions live inside the heredoc. Options:
1. **Extract to separate file** - More modular but adds files
2. **Inline test generation** - Test script extracts functions dynamically
3. **E2E only** - Skip unit tests, rely on replay/live tests

**Recommendation:** Option 2 - dynamically extract functions for testing without adding files to the distribution.

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.6.2","title":"Record HAR fixtures for replay tests","description":"## What

Record HTTP Archive (HAR) files from real sessions for deterministic replay testing.

## Why HAR Replay

- **Fast:** No network calls, runs in <5 seconds
- **Deterministic:** Same inputs → same outputs
- **Offline:** CI doesn't need internet access
- **Stable:** Immune to platform UI changes (uses recorded responses)

## Playwright HAR Recording

```javascript
// Record a session
const context = await browser.newContext({
    recordHar: { path: 'fixtures/dropbox.har' }
});
// ... perform actions ...
await context.close();  // HAR saved on close
```

## Replay in Tests

```javascript
// Replay from HAR
const context = await browser.newContext({
    har: { path: 'fixtures/dropbox.har' }
});
// Network requests will be served from HAR
```

## Fixtures to Record

| Platform | URL Type | Fixture Name |
|----------|----------|--------------|
| iCloud | Single photo | `icloud-single.har` |
| iCloud | Album | `icloud-album.har` |
| Dropbox | Single file | `dropbox-single.har` |
| Google Photos | Single photo | `gphotos-single.har` |
| Google Photos | Album | `gphotos-album.har` |
| Google Drive | Public file | `gdrive-public.har` |

## Recording Workflow

```bash
# 1. Set up recording environment
export RECORD_HAR=1

# 2. Run giil against real URLs
./giil "https://share.icloud.com/photos/xxx" --output /tmp/test

# 3. HAR is saved to scripts/fixtures/
ls scripts/fixtures/*.har
```

## Implementation

Add to extractor.mjs:
```javascript
if (process.env.RECORD_HAR) {
    const harPath = `fixtures/${detectPlatform(url)}-${Date.now()}.har`;
    context = await browser.newContext({
        recordHar: { path: harPath, mode: 'full' }
    });
    console.error(`[giil] Recording HAR to: ${harPath}`);
}
```

## CI Configuration

```yaml
replay-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - run: npm ci
    - run: npm test -- --grep "replay"
```

## Fixture Maintenance

When platforms change their CDN/UI:
1. Live tests will fail first (canary)
2. Re-record fixtures: `RECORD_HAR=1 ./giil "https://..."`
3. Update selectors/strategies if needed
4. Commit new fixtures

## Storage Considerations

HAR files can be large (1-5MB each). Options:
- Git LFS for large fixtures
- Compress with gzip (Playwright supports .har.gz)
- Store only essential responses (filter out analytics/ads)

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7","title":"EPIC: Phase 7 - Documentation & Release","description":"## Overview

Comprehensive documentation updates and v3.0.0 release preparation.

## Documentation Updates

### 1. Rebrand README
```markdown
<h1>giil</h1>
<h3>Get Image [from] Internet Link</h3>

Originally built for iCloud, now supporting:
- iCloud • Dropbox • Google Photos • Google Drive [experimental]
```

### 2. Platform Support Table
```markdown
| Platform | Method | Speed | Full Resolution |
|----------|--------|-------|-----------------|
| iCloud | Playwright (4-tier) | 5-15s | ✅ Yes |
| Dropbox | Direct URL (no browser!) | 1-3s | ✅ Yes |
| Google Photos | URL extraction + direct | 5-10s | ✅ Yes |
| Google Drive | Playwright [experimental] | 5-15s | Conditional |
```

### 3. Per-Platform Examples
Show usage for each platform with expected output.

### 4. Per-Platform Troubleshooting
- Dropbox: Password-protected, expired links
- Google Photos: Private albums, video handling
- Google Drive: "Anyone with link" requirement
- iCloud: Existing troubleshooting (unchanged)

### 5. Migration Notes
```markdown
## Upgrading from v2.x

Key changes:
- Default: Original bytes preserved (compression now opt-in)
- New JSON fields: schema_version, ok, platform
- New exit codes: 10-13, 20
- New flags: --optimize, --convert, --print-url
```

## AGENTS.md Updates

- Add new platform architecture to "Supported Platforms" section
- Update capture strategy diagram
- Document new exit codes

## Release Checklist

1. [ ] Update VERSION to 3.0.0
2. [ ] Update README with all changes
3. [ ] Update AGENTS.md
4. [ ] Create release notes template
5. [ ] Tag v3.0.0
6. [ ] Verify GitHub release artifacts
7. [ ] Update install.sh URL in README

## Success Criteria

- [ ] README reflects multi-platform support
- [ ] Each platform has usage examples
- [ ] Each platform has troubleshooting section
- [ ] Migration notes help v2 users
- [ ] v3.0.0 release published with checksums

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.1","title":"Rewrite README for multi-platform support","description":"## What

Comprehensively update README.md to reflect multi-platform support, rebrand messaging, and v3 changes.

## Header Rebrand

FROM:
```markdown
# Get iCloud Image Link
Download full-resolution images from iCloud photo shares
```

TO:
```markdown
# giil
### Get Image [from] Internet Link

Download full-resolution images from cloud sharing links.
Originally built for iCloud, now supporting Dropbox, Google Photos, and Google Drive.
```

## New Platform Support Table

```markdown
## Supported Platforms

| Platform | Method | Speed | Full Resolution | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|-----------------|-------|
| iCloud | Playwright (4-tier) | 5-15s | ✅ Always | Original and best-supported |
| Dropbox | Direct URL | 1-3s | ✅ Always | No browser needed! |
| Google Photos | URL extraction | 5-10s | ✅ Always | Uses =s0 modifier |
| Google Drive | Playwright | 5-15s | Conditional | [Experimental] Requires public sharing |
```

## Per-Platform Examples

```markdown
## Quick Examples

### iCloud
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123"
\`\`\`

### Dropbox (fastest!)
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?dl=0"
# Direct download, no browser needed
\`\`\`

### Google Photos
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123"
# Also works with album links for --all mode
\`\`\`

### Google Drive [experimental]
\`\`\`bash
giil "https://drive.google.com/file/d/xxx/view"
# File must be shared as "Anyone with the link"
\`\`\`
```

## Per-Platform Troubleshooting

```markdown
## Troubleshooting

### Dropbox
- **"Received HTML instead of image"**: Link may be password-protected or expired
- **Empty output**: Verify the file is publicly shared

### Google Photos  
- **"AUTH_REQUIRED"**: Album is private. Ask owner to make it public.
- **Low resolution**: Ensure giil is using =s0 modifier (check with --verbose)

### Google Drive
- **"File is not publicly shared"**: Owner must set "Anyone with the link" access
- **Quality warning**: Drive may return lower-resolution thumbnails as fallback
```

## v3 Changes Section

```markdown
## Upgrading from v2.x

### Breaking Changes
- **Exit codes 4 and 5 changed**: Network errors now exit 10, auth errors exit 11
- **JSON schema additions**: New fields `schema_version`, `ok`, `platform`

### Behavior Changes
- **Original bytes preserved by default**: Use `--optimize` for MozJPEG compression
- **New flags**: `--print-url`, `--verbose`, `--trace`, `--convert`, `--optimize`

### Migration
\`\`\`bash
# If you were checking exit codes:
# v2: if [ $? -eq 4 ]; then  →  v3: if [ $? -eq 10 ]; then
# v2: if [ $? -eq 5 ]; then  →  v3: if [ $? -eq 11 ]; then

# If you relied on automatic JPEG compression:
# v2: giil "https://..."
# v3: giil "https://..." --optimize
\`\`\`
```

## File Size Considerations

Current README is comprehensive. Be careful not to bloat it. Consider:
- Keep main README focused on quick-start
- Add PLATFORMS.md for deep platform-specific docs (if needed)
- Link to GitHub wiki for edge cases

@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Unit test runner for giil pure functions
+# Uses Node.js 18+ native test runner
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+TEMP_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}"
+EXTRACTED_MODULE="$TEMP_DIR/giil-pure-functions.mjs"
+
+echo "=== giil Unit Tests ==="
+echo ""
+
+# Step 1: Extract pure functions from giil
+echo "[1/3] Extracting pure functions from giil..."
+node "$SCRIPT_DIR/extract-functions.mjs" > "$EXTRACTED_MODULE"
+echo "      Extracted to: $EXTRACTED_MODULE"
+
+# Step 2: Run tests
+echo ""
+echo "[2/3] Running unit tests..."
+echo ""
+
+# Run all test files
+NODE_OPTIONS="--experimental-vm-modules" node --test "$SCRIPT_DIR"/*.test.mjs
+
+echo ""
+echo "[3/3] Cleanup..."
+rm -f "$EXTRACTED_MODULE"
+echo "      Done!"
